title: Haplocarpha rueppellii
intro: Haplocarpha rueppellii, also known as Rueppell's bush-pea or Rueppell's bushpea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in South African medicine for its purported health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Haplocarpha rueppellii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried extracts and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
